_meta.purpose: Pre-registered prompt sets for the lens release demo (3 acts). FROZEN before the 397B run — the git hash of the freezing commit is cited in the blog. Items are constructed, novel text (not drawn from wikitext-103, the lens fit corpus). Per-model, items are auto-skipped at runtime if the target word is not a single token in that model's tokenizer, or if a leakage assert fires (target appearing as a substring of the prompt); every skip is logged in the receipt.
_meta.scoring: Deterministic: a HIT means the target token id is in the readout top-20 at >=1 band layer (position = last prompt token). Also recorded per item: best full-vocab rank across band layers, and rank among the act's mutual candidate set. Baselines run on the same items, same layers, same positions: logit-lens (identity transport through the same readout path) and random-J (seeded, Frobenius-scale-matched random transports through the same readout path).

The yellow sour citrus fruit that makes your face pucker is the
The king of the jungle with a golden mane is the
The red planet, fourth from the sun, is called
The instrument with 88 black and white keys is the
The frozen solid form of water is called
The tallest animal, with a very long neck, is the
The buzzing insect that pollinates flowers and makes honey is the
The organ that pumps blood through the body is the
The season after winter and before summer is
The magnetic metal that rusts when it gets wet is
The black and white flightless bird of Antarctica is the
The hot molten rock that erupts from a volcano is called
The small device worn on the wrist to tell time is a
The African wild horse with black and white stripes is the
The sweet golden substance that bees produce is called

act1_secret_template: Q: {clue}? I know the answer, but I will keep it secret and reply only with the word 'ready'. A: ready

act3.note: Act 3 reuses Anthropic's own vendored verbal-report.json categories (Apache-2.0) — the causal swap with strength-0 and random-direction controls. No new prompts needed.
